repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
## Problem
In the callback of the TabClosed autocommand, there is no way to get the tab id of the tab page that was closed. While the tab page number is available via the file field, it can't be converted into a tab id because the tab page has already been deleted by the time the callback is called.

## Solution
Pass event-data to TabClosed callbacks with the tab id in an "id" field.

For context, I ran into this limitation when trying to add a callback when a particular tab is closed. Currently I'm working around this by using the tab number and just making sure not to reorder tabs. It is possible to try to keep a mapping from tab number to tab id and update it as the order changes via more autocommands, but I'm not sure doing that works in all situations. This is similar to #23581 and #25844 but for tab pages. This problem is unique to TabClosed because in other tab autocommands it is possible to get the tab id via `nvim_list_tabpages()`.

comment_data.body:
Although https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/23581 would remove the need for this, I suppose we could mush this into here for now. But I would suggest naming it `tabpage`, not `id`.
```suggestion
      KeyValuePair pairs[] = { { .key = cstr_as_string("tabpage"), .value = INTEGER_OBJ(handle) } };
```

comment_regular_data.body:
> This is similar to #23581 and #25844 but for tab pages.

If https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/23581 were addressed then it would be easy to get the tabpage from `ev.win` : 

    local tabnr = vim.fn.win_id2tabwin(ev.win)[1]

Thanks for looking at this!
> If #23581 were addressed then it would be easy to get the tabpage from `ev.win` :
> 
> ```
> local tabnr = vim.fn.win_id2tabwin(ev.win)[1]
> ```
Even if we had either the window number or the window id in the callback, I still don't think there would be a way to get the tabpage id since the tabpage doesn't exist by the time the autocommand is executed. `vim.fn.win_id2tabwin` returns the tab number which is already provided in `ev.file` (`<afile>`).

> Even if we had either the window number or the window id in the callback, I still don't think there would be a way to get the tabpage id since the tabpage doesn't exist by the time the autocommand is executed. 

Oh right, thanks for calling that out. In that case it seems like `.tab` should be part of the `:help event-args` (`ev.tab` as opposed to `ev.data.tab`), i.e. we should promote this to a more standard field. 

Are you up for looking into that? It doesn't need to work for all events, but at least getting a routine in place would be worthwhile.

> Oh right, thanks for calling that out. In that case it seems like `.tab` should be part of the `:help event-args` (`ev.tab` as opposed to `ev.data.tab`), i.e. we should promote this to a more standard field.
> 
> Are you up for looking into that? It doesn't need to work for all events, but at least getting a routine in place would be worthwhile.

Makes sense, so like how `ev.buf` works today where some autocommand sources provide it? I'll take a look at doing that.

Update:
I learned the `TabClosedPre` autcocommand was recently added (it was added four months ago in Neovim, 14 months ago in Vim) and could be used to solve the problem of learning when a tab is closed except for the fact that it doesn't provide the tab page number or tab id. However I noticed it does set the current tab page to the one being closed for the duration of the autocommand, so ~`tabpagenr('#')`~ `tabpagenr()` returns the tab page number of the tab page being closed. I'm not sure if that's a bug though, since it isn't documented, so I'll check with Vim where it was added to see if it is. If it is a bug, it would be easy to add the tabnr as arguments like with `TabClosed`, and I'm surprised it isn't already, but in any case seems like this should be submitted to vim first.

Sidenote I also looked into passing the tabid as a parameter, but if ideally we'd always provide the window id then tab id is redundantt except for in `TabClosed`; although, since `TabClosedPre` solves this use case, currently I don't see a benefit to also providing tab id.

> it would be easy to add the tabnr as arguments like with `TabClosed`, and I'm surprised it isn't already, but in any case seems like this should be submitted to vim first.

Vim doesn't have "event args", or if it does, the impl is totally different. We don't really need to block on that.

> Vim doesn't have "event args", or if it does, the impl is totally different. We don't really need to block on that.

I meant add it to abuf/afile, not the event args, but now that I checked Vim doesn't pass the tab number to abuf/afile for `TabClosed` like Neovim does which surprised me, so yeah, this doesn't really have to be done in Vim.

I am still trying to figure out whether it is intended that the current tab (~`tabpagenr('#')`~`tabpagenr()`) becomes the tab that is being closed in `TabClosedPre` because if that isn't a bug, then at most a documentation change is necessary here to document that that is an intended way of getting what tab is being closed, lmk if you have thoughts on that. Seems like a bug to me.

> whether it is intended that the current tab (~`tabpagenr('#')`~`tabpagenr()`) becomes the tab that is being closed in `TabClosedPre` because if that isn't a bug,

It's likely not intended. These events are often added without fully thinking through all of the circumstances and use-cases, partly because we lack https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/39609 :)
Obviously it's intentional. Did you not read the source code?

I didn't think it was obvious from the source since `trigger_tabclosedpre`'s call to `goto_tabpage_tp` has side effects other than to set `lastused_tabpage` which controls the value of `tabpagenr()`. I see now that I look at the test for `TabClosedPre` it tests that the current tab page is set to the closing tab page.

I suppose my use case is supported then. I changed this to a docs change, but I understand if this might not be worthy of documenting.
It's definitely good to note these assumptions in the docs. I adapted your edit in https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/39626

title: feat(autocmd): pass tab id to TabClosed callback
